_patch_meta.description: Re-evaluation of treachery G4273 prodotēs (mti_term_id 1365) — no changes recommended. All 3 verses (Luk 6:16, Acts 7:52, 2 Tim 3:4) and 1 active group confirmed correct under v3_5: 1365-001 names the inner-being characteristic of treachery as a defining moral quality of the inner person; the agent-noun morphology of prodotēs makes calling someone a 'traitor' an attribution of settled inner moral disposition. 2 Tim 3:4 retained as anchor — the vice-list context (alongside reckless, conceited, pleasure-loving) makes the inner-being weight explicit standalone. Luk 6:16 (Judas characterised) and Acts 7:52 (Stephen's audience characterised) retained as related. Group description reads as characteristic-perspective per §6.2 Step 3; anchor distribution satisfies R4.
